schedule.tip: As Infosys's process includes a behavioral HR interview, be prepared to clearly articulate your background and foundational programming knowledge like OOPS concepts.

tasks.title: What is the difference between Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+C?

schedule.tip: Infosys's online assessment requires a qualifying percentage of 65%, suggesting that accuracy in understanding even basic technical distinctions is important.

tasks.title: Practice MCQs covering basic programming concepts

schedule.tip: Infosys's online assessment includes MCQs, so dedicating time to practice these will help you meet the required qualifying percentage.

tasks.title: Solve a basic programming question (e.g., string manipulation)

schedule.tip: The online assessment features two programming questions, so practicing solving these will be directly beneficial for Round 1.

tasks.title: Describe your experience using Tosca and its common applications.
Prepare STAR method answers for common behavioral questions.

schedule.tip: Infosys's HR interview often delves into your resume and projects; be ready to discuss specific tools like Tosca and provide concrete examples of your experience.

tasks.title: Revisit OOPS concepts and their real-world applications.
Review answers for 'Tell me about yourself' and other introductory questions.

schedule.tip: Given that Infosys looks for both technical aptitude and behavioral fit, refining your answers to core questions ensures a strong impression in both rounds.

tasks.title: Practice a mix of MCQs and a simple programming question

schedule.tip: Infosys's online assessment is a significant hurdle, so practicing both MCQ and coding components simulates the actual test environment.

tasks.title: Practice answering behavioural questions aloud, focusing on clarity and conciseness.

schedule.tip: Infosys's HR interview is designed to assess your communication skills and suitability for the role, making practice of behavioral answers crucial.

tasks.title: Deep dive into experiences related to Tosca and its integration into projects.

schedule.tip: Be prepared to elaborate on your experience with specific tools like Tosca, as Infosys values candidates who can demonstrate practical application of their skills.

tasks.title: Full Mock Interview (Round 1 focus: MCQs and Programming)

schedule.tip: Simulating the online assessment with a mock interview helps identify areas for improvement before the actual Infosys assessment, where a 65% qualifying percentage is key.
Practicing the HR interview with a mock session allows you to refine your responses to behavioral questions and ensure a confident presentation to the Infosys interviewer.

tasks.title: Review and practice all listed behavioral questions thoroughly.
Practice articulating project experiences and technical skills.

schedule.tip: Infosys's interview process heavily relies on behavioral assessment, so dedicating a day to comprehensively practice all potential behavioral questions is essential.

tasks.title: Review all behavioral questions and key concepts.
Quick practice of basic programming question types.

schedule.tip: A final revision ensures that you are well-prepared for both the technical MCQs and programming questions in the Infosys online assessment, as well as the behavioral aspects of the HR interview.
While Infosys's interview involves assessment, ensuring you are well-rested and mentally prepared will help you perform your best on the day.
